texto: How do you know when it's over?" "Maybe when you feel more in love with your memories than with the person standing in front of you.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gunnar Ardelius que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “How do you know when it's over?" texto: In the beginning, being alone is always a choice. Then it's not a choice anymore. When did it stop being a choice? What is it in me that stopped choosing you, that moved into you instead so that I have to be with you in order to be with myself?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gunnar Ardelius que provoca temas ligados a amor. Ao questionar “In the beginning, being alone is always a choice. Then it's not a choice anymore. When did it sto…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, alone, romance, com foco em romance.

frase_original: In the beginning, being alone is always a choice. Then it's not a choice anymore. When did it stop being a choice? What is it in me that stopped choosing you, that moved into you instead so that I have to be with you in order to be with myself?

texto: It frightens me that I can't do anything sensible about it.""Are you scared that you'll wind up with a boring job where you have to see the same people every day and drink instant coffee?""I'm more scared that I'll forget the feeling I have now.""Kind of like how you forgot how it feels to be three years old.""That surely I'll wind up thinking I was so young, I didn't really understand everything. It bothers me that I know I will be wrong.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gunnar Ardelius: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It frightens me that I can't do anything sensible about it.""Are you scared that you'll wind up w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, memories, romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de romance e young.

frase_original: It frightens me that I can't do anything sensible about it.""Are you scared that you'll wind up with a boring job where you have to see the same people every day and drink instant coffee?""I'm more scared that I'll forget the feeling I have now.""Kind of like how you forgot how it feels to be three years old.""That surely I'll wind up thinking I was so young, I didn't really understand everything. It bothers me that I know I will be wrong.
